item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly this felt rather lukewarm to me and none of the things other people have stated as blowing it out of the water for them really did it for me, if anything some of them just intensified the lukewarmness of the article.

It's well written and structured but yeah no oomf.

And as above with tapnek the final addendum just makes me wonder what kind of grudge these bozos have. SCP 1K at least had an explanation that left your morals in a twist, on one side the Foundation insisting that if these things ever become a power again they'll do to us what we did to them and on the other (personally my humanitarian instinct) the desperate plea for mercy and promise of peace form SCP 1k.

These guys though… They're abducting, lobotomizing and torturing humans to keep as domestic animals as some form of vengeance for an act or war that either was committed by a now long extinct civilization or culture or a violent para-military group that isn't representative of humanity as a whole (like the GoC likes shooting anomalies on sight right?). So really they're kinda just vengeful tools rather than sympathetic victims of human cruelty. There's also the fact we have no idea what we(?) did to them to deserve such disdain and foul treatment of entirely innocent passerbys who were just in the wrong place at the wrong time.

S'just me though, aside from that it's a solid piece, nothing really wrong with it that sticks out to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What did we do to these humanoids exactly? The Foundation doesn't seem to know much about them. From the way they described our actions to us, it sounds like SCP-1000 but bigger and stronger and with more sophisticated technology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This scip is going to give me more nightmares than SCP-106. I think it's because of the combination of lobotomy, all the info from the website, &

How to Identify False Obedience.pdf

TAKE ALL OF MY UPVOTES & help me please D;
I enjoyed this one. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't sell me until the final addendum. It's absolutely a sinister message, and really damn creepy. The article going from "horrifying and weird" to "silly to weird" lost me because of how much those two parts seemed separate, so that addendum helped nail the underlying tone throughout and greatly made me appreciate this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a valid complaint. It's not so much about the super-humans as it is about what they're doing, so I wanted to get them out of the way to get to the "meat" of the SCP. I could probably have executed that better, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept of aliens, (humanoids, whatever), with american accents grabbing people first comes off as odd, if not humorous, the article takes a darker twist. The list of pdfs is what really shows the twisted nature of what's going on here to me.

Nice work. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. It just didn't grab me like it seemed to grab everyone else. The popping in and out of reality, the poor descriptions of the super-humans beyond 'they're big and powerful' - these things did nothing for me. The general idea was okay, but was a bit predictably consistent throughout - that is, I figured out what was going on quite early, and nothing really surprised or excited me thereafter. I liked the 'false obedience' line, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well written, and very unsettling. What creeped me out the most is the months-old child with his/her face contorted into a smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cannot help but notice that both in reality and in fiction, every intelligent species, as a whole, seems to think it is better than all other species, the most important, the most deserving of what they want, etcetra.
This is, of course, a bigoted, self-absorbed, and altogether ridiculous assumption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, very creepy. Well written in its current form!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sure is one of the creepiest new SCPs I've seen in a while. Made me twitch nervously in my chair.
Well done, have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delightfully disturbing. +1.
Oh god! What if SCP-1897 met SCP-962?! Someone needs to write that tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I hadn't already upvoted, this would've made me do so. Outstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jam son. "Lol, humans as pets" didn't do much for me on its own, but… wow. The tone of that note was seriously condescendingly sinister. Changed to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Due to pretty much everybody wanting the expungement removed, I removed it, and revealed what's behind it. I hope that gets me a ton of upvotes makes things better for everybody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only issue I have with this SCP is the last expungement. While I like old school expungements like these (similar to the old gate to hell and the plague doctor), I believe that it should reveal at least a bit, which is something the modern SCP standards adhere to.

I already +1d, though. I like it very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I don't think he really wanted to discuss his reaction, guy was just impressed by the SCP. I remember how I was freaked out / impressed / just scared shitless when I began lurking here. Not everybody has to reply in the same bland way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a thoroughly creepy SCP. Like, deep-bone creepy, very subtle and calm at first, and then the Batshit Freakout goes to 11, and you get a +1. All the ideas and questions this thing raises, and the obvious "What would I do?" that likely all of us thought of reading this is just great. Supremely well done article, I wish we had more of this depth, complexity, and detail. Gold star.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first new SCP I've read since I've been off of here these past few weeks, and I like it a lot. I like concept and everything, it's very appealing to me. I like the idea that we humans are "dog" size in comparison to these things.

As human cubs are always in high demand

O.O I like that line too.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keep in mind that discussion threads are meant for discussing the article, not necessarily just your specific reactions to them (some of which can be attributed to various articles). I'm not sure how much the rest of the site needs to know about your need to take pills.

(also, the two people who told you to rein it in a little are a senior staff member and an admin, respectively)

Edit: Saw your recent comment; thank you for understanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay,let's calm down and see.Bright idea,make readers feel creepy,perfect horror points——However,a very good SCP.
Althrough I really want to [DATA EXPUNGED] for making us feel chilly and may cause nightmares or something worser.
But,it's really an amazing one.+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You need a cup of 17℃ water,I think.
And I need to tell my self "All these above are are just god damn fictions" and take some pills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude. Chill.

Like, all the way down.

Maybe switch to decaf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Calm down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought about an idea that if I become a pet of some greater creature(such as giants or aliens) since I was four and imaged about the food and habit…
And now I amazed by this!
OK,I've never think about all these you wrote.Good job and jkgfcdtrohjyufdgyuhjl;jkhgfdhjyuklonmhjvgfju';[p

SHOULD I UPVOTE THIS OR KILL THE AUTHOR?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny fact:

There is an entire subgenre of BDSM pornography that is more or less this, but with less aliens, less neurosugery (though mind-altering shenanigans are often present or at least implied) and more weird sex.

The part of the site that didn't already know this would still want you to not post pictures in the forums.- Scantron

It's supremely creepy. Which, I guess, is upvote worthy. Have your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Humans being used in the same way horses and dogs are in this reality? Sounds suitably horrifying to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This disturbs me more than you know…but I like it. I definitely want to know more!!! Great idea and great execution. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In this context, the more accurate term would be confusing.
I for one would wonder why they bothered to list a medieval melee implement specifically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea. Very well executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole time I was reading this I felt just a little bit unsettled. Then I read this:

Coming in June : Say No to Skin! A Panel on How You Can Help Prevent Unauthorized Human Poaching and Skinning

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pepper spray

Mace is the correct term for spray cans of that kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A banner across the front page declares the website as belonging to the "Human Domestication Society" in standard American English.

This was the moment I +1'd

How to Identify False Obedience

This was where I decided to comment.

Don't get me wrong, this is an excellent article, but the "events" section almost kills it. You're going from images of abduction and torture to people pulling sleds, jumping from extremely serious to extremely silly. This juxtaposition can work, but not in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhhh. The writing isn't bad, but the entities (which are really poorly defined here) taking humans as pets act exactly like humans, which renders this a pretty straightforward "role-reversal" scenario. I would like this a lot better if the setup were something much weirder perpetrating this, written so that the "people as pets" thing was some sort of fridge-horror realization that the reader gets as they put the pieces together.

Also, I don't think lobotomies work like that. Everything I've read on the subject leads me to believe that lobotomizing someone brings about profound changes in behavior and character, but it doesn't seem to have a direct effect on intelligence, per se. It's not like you stick an icepick in someone's eyeball and they're all like "DURR HURR me have reduced intelligence". So when the article talks about lobotomizing people to the point where they display intelligence equal to that of a dog, I'm not buying it.

The "human resources" thing made me laugh, though.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noooo, the issue here is that these guys lobotomize us in order to keep us as pets, and then torture us into obedience. I don't think the point of this article at all was that pet ownership was bad. Not unless you've given your dog a lobotomy so it doesn't speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see what this is trying to be, and it's well written and gets the concept across well, but to me it reads like a hamfisted PETA tract ("Put yourself in the ANIMAL'S POSITION IT'S NASTY ISN'T IT!") and that outweighs the SCP-ness of it for me.

No vote from me, but that's because of my biases, not anything inherent in the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a lot better. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like that last note.

Given that there isn't much overt description of SCP-1897 beyond "big humanoids in camo", part of me is excitedly wondering if we're finally seeing the dimension-hopping human-haters in the flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeeeugh, I love this. Take my upvote, please!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that part was pretty awkward. Is it better now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This line confuses me:

The individual must appear to be alone or with a group of fewer than two other individuals.

Is this the same as saying: "The person must be either alone or with at most one other individual" ?

Clearer still: "The person must be in a group of no more than two."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Before I forget, I'd like to thank everybody in chat and in the forums that helped me with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How to Introduce Your Human to Other Humans Humans naturally don't get along, but we can help you with that.

I admit this one made me chuckle, but I didn't finish to read the article yet, so I'll hold my vote till see the entire skip.

E: I can imagine this skip working in reality, and I must say is good enough for me to upvote (despite the execution issues). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, that is exactly what I was trying to say! And thank you for the praise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much better with the expungements removed. Also:

the reduced intelligence is a result of the severing of connections both to and from the prefrontal cortex if not more, it cannot be treated in the majority of cases

might be better phrased as

is a result of a partial lobotomy of the prefrontal cortex and selective damage to Broca's area, neither of which can be repaired by non-anomalous means at this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everybody in chat wanted me to get rid of expunges as well. Let's see what's behind all but one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember he jumped through all or most of the necessary hoops in chat, I liked the concept, will read at lunch

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thirding about the execution difficulties, particularly the expungement. In addition, it fails to explain exactly how SCP-1897-2 is related to SCP-1897, and why there just so happens to be exactly one website dedicated to discussing humans in terms of their domestication.

And on the whole, anything that might have been interesting is removed, and what's left is just far too obvious and one-dimensional. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, excessive expungements abound and this tone could possibly use some touching up here and there.

However.

How to Identify False Obedience

Say No to Skin! A Panel on How You Can Help Prevent Unauthorized Human Poaching and Skinning

Subject : Enough

Please try to understand that this is for your own good.

The above excerpts from the article are lines that stuck out to me as being outstandingly interesting/creepy/well-done along with the rest of the article, and the good vastly outweighs the bad here, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The states are New Mexico, Arizona, Texas, Colorado, and Nevada, aren't they?

Beyond that nitpick that any moron with a passable knowledge of American geography can guess the redacted information, I liked this idea.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of The Fantastic Planet, a 1973 French/Czech co-production animated science-fiction film.

Regardless, I enjoy this article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is very solid. Usage of the words such as kennels, and the lack of expression on their faces were nice. However, there's a good bit of cockblock expungement. Neutral vote until revised with a slightly more professional tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that the expungement is iffy. Especially blackboxing the states. You already said that this is located in the US Southwest, so the states in question aren't exactly hard to figure out from the number of blackboxes in each word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like Roget said, execution is spotty but this is damn solid for a first SCP. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Proper [DATA EXPUNGED] Usage

I suspect you should have read this section of their website. Seriously," menacing with a large electrified [DATA EXPUNGED]" made me think of electro-cocks for some reason, and there went all attempts to maintain seriousness and achieve any impact.

Other than that, this is somewhat better than the draft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's certainly… different. I can see this becoming popular, but I can also see it becoming a huge flop. No vote for now while I form a better opinion on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm. It's sort of a novel idea, but the presentation is iffy. Some of the description(blackboxing the speed, "lifting an adult human with a single hand.", "immediately ceases to exist on this plane") needs work, and the way you describe the domestication was sort of bleah. The notes almost save it, but they don't go far enough into the weird zone to hold the piece. The overuse of expungement is probably hurting this the most, especially since it seems to be withholding the most interesting information. Neutral vote for now, but I'd like to see an improved version of this. Definitely better than most other first SCP attempts.
